# Prerelease Gate — 2026-07-10

## Summary
- Repo: danieljustus/symaira-memory
- HEAD: 242bfea47f5e4e76d1656c7a0cdb9ea16b6f6a6d
- Last release: v0.11.1
- Proposed target: v0.12.0 (bump: minor)
- Gate: blocked

## Gate checks
| Check | Result | Detail |
|---|---|---|
| CI / tests (HEAD) | PASS | GitHub Actions run 29115612344 completed `success` on HEAD 242bfea |
| Version consistency | PASS | 3 stale version mentions checked, 3 corrected in working tree |

| Patch coverage | WARN | 50.8% of changed lines tested (default 80% patch bar) |

| Security | PASS | 0 open critical (Dependabot alerts disabled; code-scanning has no analysis) |

## Findings
- [ ] **[BLOCK][Docs] README and agent guide omit context profiles feature**
  - **Status quo:** New context profile feature (#337) adds `cmd/context_profile.go` CLI commands, `internal/db/context_profiles.go` DB layer, migration `021_context_profiles.sql`, and an optional `profile` parameter on `memory_search` in `internal/mcp/mcp_tools.go`. README features list does not mention context profiles; `docs/agent-integration.md` `memory_search` arguments omit `profile`.
  - **Likely cause:** Feature landed after README/agent-integration.md were last updated.
  - **Proposed solution:** Add a README feature bullet for context profiles and document the `profile` parameter in the MCP tools table.
  - **Handoff:** `/02-gh-plan --from-review` or `/03-gh-go`

- [ ] **[BLOCK][Docs] README omits benchmark/evaluation command**
  - **Status quo:** New `symmemory bench` command (#336) adds `cmd/bench.go`, the `internal/bench` package, and corpus-backed retrieval evaluation (Recall@k, NDCG@k, MRR, latency percentiles). README features list and quickstart do not mention it.
  - **Likely cause:** Benchmark was treated as internal tooling; it is a user-facing CLI command.
  - **Proposed solution:** Add a README feature bullet and a quickstart example for `symmemory bench`.
  - **Handoff:** `/02-gh-plan --from-review` or `/03-gh-go`

- [ ] **[WARN][Docs] PII Guard documentation list is incomplete**
  - **Status quo:** #335 expanded PII redaction to URL credentials, vendor tokens (GitLab, npm, GitHub app/refresh, Firebase, HTTP Basic Auth, Docker config auth), and a high-entropy fallback. README line 198 and `docs/agent-integration.md` lines 186-190 still list only credit cards, email addresses, and API tokens.
  - **Likely cause:** Security feature expansion not mirrored in user-facing docs.
  - **Proposed solution:** Update README and `docs/agent-integration.md` PII lists to match the new redaction categories.
  - **Handoff:** `/02-gh-plan --from-review` or `/03-gh-go`

- [ ] **[WARN][Docs] `.goreleaser.yml` Homebrew license mismatches repo license**
  - **Status quo:** `.goreleaser.yml:56` sets `license: MIT` for the Homebrew formula, but the repository is Apache-2.0 (README license badge and `LICENSE` file). This publishes incorrect license metadata to the tap.
  - **Likely cause:** Copy-paste from another project or stale config.
  - **Proposed solution:** Change `.goreleaser.yml` `brews[].license` to `Apache-2.0`.
  - **Handoff:** `/03-gh-go` or manual fix

- [ ] **[WARN][Coverage] Patch coverage 50.8% below default 80% patch bar**
  - **Status quo:** `v0.11.1..HEAD` diff is 1550 added lines; 788 are covered. Largest gaps: `cmd/context_profile.go` 14.3%, `cmd/bench.go` 11.1%, `SearchMemoriesWithProfile` 0.0%, `SearchWithProfile` 0.0%.
  - **Likely cause:** New CLI command wiring and end-to-end profile search paths lack tests.
  - **Proposed solution:** Add unit/integration tests for context profile CLI commands and profile-aware search.
  - **Handoff:** `/test-coverage` → `/02-gh-plan --from-coverage .github/coverage/2026-07-10T19-20-50Z.md` → `/03-gh-go`

- [ ] **[WARN][Coverage] Overall 67.2% below default 80% bar**
  - **Status quo:** Overall statement coverage is 67.2%; no repo-configured threshold exists. Lowest high-risk modules: `internal/mcp/auth.go` 33.3%, `internal/db/db.go` 29.2%.
  - **Likely cause:** Pre-existing gaps; delta +1.4 shows improvement versus the last release.
  - **Proposed solution:** Prioritize tests for auth and DB init paths.
  - **Handoff:** `/test-coverage` → `/02-gh-plan --from-coverage .github/coverage/2026-07-10T19-20-50Z.md` → `/03-gh-go`

- [ ] **[WARN][Tests/Perf] 16 tests over 500ms slow the suite**
  - **Status quo:** With `-race -p 1`, suite wall time is ~300s. Top 3 tests: `TestEmbeddingBackupSize` 79.9s, `TestEmbeddingStorageSize` 67.2s, `TestEmbeddingRecommendation` 47.6s.
  - **Likely cause:** Embedding tests perform real I/O or heavy computation without isolated boundaries.
  - **Proposed solution:** Refactor embedding tests to use test doubles or smaller fixtures; separate heavy integration tests.
  - **Handoff:** `/02-gh-plan --from-review` or `/03-gh-go`
